plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-491 RP16.P04.M03.S21-RP16.P04.M05.S16 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-491 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP16.P04 workflow permission slice: the M03 Primary Implementation Slice tail (the two remaining rows), the full M04 Secondary Workflow Slice cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M05 Permission And Audit Binding head (the first sixteen rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H16), Claude review packet (C16), and CP00-492 handoff, without opening governance, DLP, legal hold, or retention runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-491 is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only governance pack (RP16.P04.M03.S21-RP16.P04.M05.S16, 40 units, risk B). Coverage, deliverable distribution (ui:18, implementation:11, claude_review:3, security_audit:4, fixture:2, test:1, hermes_evidence:1) and micro-title distribution (2/22/16) match exactly across the baseline, manifest, GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP491_REQUIREMENTS, the coverage validator, and the live closeout-pack-plan, which lists CP00-491 as the head of the live queue. Pack continuity is correct: CP00-490 hands off into RP16.P04.M03.S21 and CP00-491 hands off to CP00-492 at RP16.P04.M05.S17. The pack-level claim is production_ready:false / implemented_pending_review with no over-claim; production_ready_candidate:true is only a descriptor-acceptance flag (coverage.valid && slice.valid). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensively attested in registry, service (freezeCp491Result), and manifest (no governance/DLP/legal-hold/retention runtime, no state/audit/DB/object-storage writes, no Hermes runtime receipt; no_real_data:true). The authority boundary is intact and enforced by both the validator and three substantive model.test.js tests: claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (tools Read/Grep/Glob, read_only:true) and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor packet/receipt handling (receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false). The CP178+ forbidden_review_evidence guard covers auth failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing refs, and final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims. Changed-file scope is accurate with no leakage: the two dirty control-room files and the LDIP invalid-attempt artifact are correctly classified unrelated. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection, receipt, raw-output) are absent by design at this review step. This review used only read-only tools (no Bash, no writes). No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-491 governance core p04 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
